message:
perf(core): in-transaction fast path for prepared UPDATE/DELETE — skip 50ns ceremony

Mirror the INSERT fast path (bd-pktso) for UPDATE/DELETE operations.
When executing a prepared UPDATE/DELETE within an explicit BEGIN..COMMIT:
- Skip clear_table_program_error_state() (no previous error)
- Skip sync_change_tracking_context() (within same txn)
- Skip refresh_memdb_from_active_txn_if_dirty() (B-tree is source)
- Skip 6+ tracing/mode checks that are always false in bulk DML

Jump directly to execute_precompiled_prepared_update_or_delete_fast()
which handles the actual VDBE execution with minimal overhead.

For 10K-row UPDATE: saves ~50ns × 10K = ~0.5ms.
For 5K-row DELETE: saves ~50ns × 5K = ~0.25ms.

Combined with the B-tree tracing fix (with_btree_op), UPDATE/DELETE
save ~150ns per row (50ns ceremony + 100ns B-tree tracing).

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
